questions.text: What is the half-life of a radioactive sample (in minutes), if its mean life is 200s?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: According to the formula and the calculations, this option represents an incorrect answer.
According to the formula and the calculations, this option represents an incorrect answer.
According to the formula and the calculations, this option represents an incorrect answer.
Mean life (τ) and half-life (T₁/₂) are related by the formula:T₁/₂ = τ × ln(2) ≈ τ × 0.693τ = 200 sT₁/₂ = 200 × 0.693 = 138.6 secondsConvert to minutes:138.6 ÷ 60 ≈ 2.31 minutes

questions.explanation: Mean life (τ) and half-life (T₁/₂) are related by the formula:T₁/₂ = τ × ln(2) ≈ τ × 0.693τ = 200 sT₁/₂ = 200 × 0.693 = 138.6 secondsConvert to minutes:138.6 ÷ 60 ≈ 2.31 minutes

questions.text: What will happen in a time of 7 hours, if a radioactive substance has an average life of 7 hours?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Half of the active nuclei decay
options.explanation: This option suggests that the amount of decay is equivalent to the half-life, which is not the same as the average life.

options.text: Less than half of the active nuclei decay
options.explanation: This option incorrectly implies that the decay is less significant than what occurs over the average life, which is not the case.

options.text: More than half of the active nuclei decay
options.explanation: Since the average life of a radioactive substance is longer than its half-life, more than half of the active nuclei will decay in the time equivalent to the average life.
This option suggests complete decay, which is not possible in the time span of one average life.

questions.explanation: The average life of a radioactive substance is the reciprocal of the decay constant, and it is longer than the half-life. The half-life is the time taken for half of the radioactive nuclei to decay. In the given scenario, the time duration is equal to the average life, which means more than half of the radioactive nuclei would have decayed. Option C is correct because it accurately reflects the decay amount over the average life span. Option A is incorrect because it describes the half-life scenario, not the average life. Option B is incorrect as it underestimates the decay, and Option D is incorrect because not all nuclei will decay in this time frame.

questions.text: Which two nuclie contain the same number of neutrons?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Let's calculate:₆¹²C → Neutrons = 12 − 6 = 6₆¹⁴C → Neutrons = 14 − 6 = 8 → Not sameHence, it's incorrect.
Let's calculate:₁₁²³Na → Neutrons = 23 − 11 = 12₁₂²⁴Mg → Neutrons = 24 − 12 = 12 → SameHence, it's correct.
Let's calculate:₇¹⁶N → Neutrons = 16 − 7 = 9₈¹⁸O → Neutrons = 18 − 8 = 10 → Not sameHence, it's incorrect.
Let's calculate:₁₄³²Si → Neutrons = 32 − 14 = 18₁₅³²P → Neutrons = 32 − 15 = 17 → Not sameHence, it's incorrect.

questions.explanation: Both nuclei have 12 neutrons, even though their atomic numbers differ.Number of neutrons in Na2311=23-11=12Number of neutrons in Mg2412=24-12=12

questions.text: Neutron decay in the free space is given in the equation below:10n → 11H + 0-1e + [ ]The parentheses represent an:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A photon is a type of elementary particle that is the quantum carrier of the electromagnetic force. Photons are particles of light, and they have no mass or electric charge. They travel at the speed of light and can behave both as a particle and as a wave.

A neutrino is a type of elementary particle that is similar to an electron, but has no electric charge and very little mass. Neutrinos are produced in a variety of natural and artificial processes, such as nuclear reactions in the sun, nuclear power plants, and particle accelerators.

A graviton is a hypothetical elementary particle that is believed to mediate the force of gravity in the theory of quantum gravity. According to this theory, gravity is thought to be carried by particles called gravitons, which interact with each other and with matter to produce the force of gravity.

Neutron decay is a type of radioactive decay that occurs when a neutron inside the nucleus of an atom spontaneously decays into a proton, an electron, and an antineutrino. In free space, this process can be represented by the following equation:10n → 11H + 0-1e + ν̄eIn this equation, "n" represents the neutron, "p" represents the proton, "e-" represents the electron, and "ν̄e" represents the antineutrino. The antineutrino is a particle that has no electric charge and very little mass, and it is emitted along with the electron during the decay process.

questions.explanation: Neutron decay is a type of radioactive decay that occurs when a neutron inside the nucleus of an atom spontaneously decays into a proton, an electron, and an antineutrino. In free space, this process can be represented by the following equation:10n → 11H + 0-1e + ν̄eIn this equation, "n" represents the neutron, "p" represents the proton, "e-" represents the electron, and " ν̄e" represents the antineutrino.

questions.text: The ratio of the rate of decay of a parent atom to the number of radioactive nuclei present at that time is equal to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Half-life is the time for half of the radioactive nuclei to decay, not a ratio involving decay rate and number of nuclei.
Mean life is the average time a radioactive nucleus remains active, not a ratio as described.

The ratio of the rate of decay of a parent atom to the number of radioactive nuclei present at that time is equal to its decay constant. According to the derivation, following can be concluded:dN/dt = −λNλ = -dN/dt / N
Activity is the rate of decay of radioactive nuclei, not a ratio involving decay rate and number of nuclei.

questions.explanation: Let the initial number of atoms at time t=0 be N0.Let N be the number of atoms at any instant t.λ is the disintegration constant.We can write that:dN/dt = −λNλ = -dN/dt / N
hints: ratio of the rate of decay

questions.text: Which one of the following can be preferred for the treatment of skin?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Beta particles are high-energy electrons (β-) or positrons (β+). They have relatively low penetration power and can be used for the treatment of superficial skin conditions, including certain types of skin cancer and dermatological conditions. Beta radiation is generally used when the condition is localized near the skin's surface, as it does not penetrate deeply into tissues.

X-rays are electromagnetic radiation with higher energy than visible light. X-rays can penetrate into tissues, and they are often used in medical imaging (X-ray imaging). However, for skin treatment, X-rays are typically used for more significant or deeper conditions, and they may not be the first choice for superficial skin treatments.
ϒ-radiation can be preferred for the treatment of skin. It consists of high energy photons and is highly penetrating.

Alpha particles (α-rays) are relatively heavy, charged particles consisting of two protons and two neutrons. They have limited penetration power and are typically not used for skin treatment. Alpha radiation is more appropriate for internal radiation therapy or brachytherapy.

questions.explanation: ϒ-radiation can be preferred for the treatment of skin. Gamma radiation consists of high-energy photons and is highly penetrating.

questions.text: Radio-isotope Cobalt which emits high-energy γ-rays is used for the treatment of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Cobalt-60 is not used for the treatment of fever. Fever is typically a symptom of an underlying medical condition and is treated with medications or other therapies aimed at addressing the root cause of the fever.

Cobalt-60 is not used for the treatment of liver conditions directly. The liver may be affected by cancer, and in some cases, radiation therapy with high-energy gamma rays (from sources like Co-60) can be used to treat liver cancer. However, Co-60 is not a specific treatment for the liver itself.

Cobalt-60 is not commonly used for the treatment of bones, although radiation therapy is sometimes used to treat bone-related conditions, such as bone metastases from cancer. In such cases, high-energy gamma radiation may be employed, but it is not specific to Coba-60.

Cobalt-60 is primarily used in radiation therapy for the treatment of cancer. High-energy gamma rays emitted by Co-60 can be precisely directed to cancerous tissues to destroy or damage cancer cells, helping to treat various types of cancer, including those in the brain, head and neck, and other parts of the body. In summary, Cobalt-60 is primarily used for the treatment of cancer through radiation therapy, making option ''D " the correct choice.

questions.explanation: Cobalt-60 is primarily used in radiation therapy for the treatment of cancer. High-energy gamma rays emitted by Co-60 can be precisely directed to cancerous tissues to destroy or damage cancer cells, helping to treat various types of cancer, including those in the brain, head and neck, and other parts of the body. In summary, Cobalt-60 is primarily used for the treatment of cancer through radiation therapy, making option ''D'' the correct choice.

questions.text: A beta particle is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A beta particle is not a Helium nucleus (alpha particle). Beta particles are different from alpha particles. Beta particles can be either electrons (beta-minus, denoted as β-) or positrons (beta-plus, denoted as β+).
Beta particles are not elements themselves but rather subatomic particles. They are associated with certain types of radioactive decay, but they are not elements.

This is the correct description. Beta particles can be either electrons (beta-minus, β-) or positrons (beta-plus, β+), which are antiparticles of electrons. In beta-minus decay, an unstable nucleus emits an electron, while in beta-plus decay, it emits a positron.

Beta particles can be negatively charged, but they are specifically electrons or positrons. Other negative particles, such as muons or tauons, are different particles with distinct properties.In summary, a beta particle is either an electron (beta-minus) or a positron (beta-plus), and it is associated with certain types of radioactive decay processes involving the emission of these particles from unstable atomic nuclei.

questions.explanation: Option C is correct because β particles are the fast moving electrons or positrons which come out of the nucleus of a radioactive element.
